title: issue1
close_reason: Test issue, replaced by structured project planning

title: Pi Agent CCCC Bridge Plugin
description: A pi-agent extension that connects running pi sessions to a CCCC daemon, bridging incoming CCCC chat messages into the agent's conversation loop in real-time. See tasks/plan.md for full architecture and decisions.

title: Set up project environment (TypeScript, linting, type checking)
description: Set up the extension directory structure with TypeScript, strict type checking, and linting. The extension lives in ~/.pi/agent/extensions/cccc-bridge/ as a directory with package.json, tsconfig, and the extension source.
design:
Directory structure:
~/.pi/agent/extensions/cccc-bridge/
  package.json       (deps: cccc-sdk, devDeps: typescript, @types/node, eslint, @typescript-eslint)
  tsconfig.json      (strict, target ES2022, module ESNext, moduleResolution bundler)
  eslint.config.mjs  (flat config, @typescript-eslint)
  src/
    index.ts         (extension entry point, default export factory)
    client.ts        (CCCC daemon client wrapper)
    actor.ts         (actor registration and identity)
    inbox.ts         (polling and delivery)
    config.ts        (env var and .pi config reading)
    types.ts         (shared types)

Follow the pi with-deps example pattern for package.json shape with the pi.extensions field.
Use ESM (type: module). Target the pi-agent extension API (@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent).
acceptance_criteria:
Extension directory exists at ~/.pi/agent/extensions/cccc-bridge/ with package.json declaring cccc-sdk as dependency
tsconfig.json with strict mode, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes enabled
ESLint configured with @typescript-eslint/recommended-type-checked
npm install --ignore-scripts succeeds
tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors
eslint passes with zero errors
notes:
## What was set up

### Toolchain
- Vite Plus v0.2.5 installed via `mise use -g viteplus`
- Monorepo scaffold at repo root: `vp create vite:monorepo` (root files carried over; "create default app" sub-step fails in v0.2.5, irrelevant)
- Extension library scaffold: `vp create vite:library` in `packages/extension/`
- Dependencies managed via pnpm catalog in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`

### Configuration
All config is consolidated into a SINGLE root `vite.config.ts`:
- `lint.options.typeAware: true` + `typeCheck: true` (type-aware oxlint + tsc)
- `staged { "*": "vp check --fix" }` (pre-commit hook runs format+lint+type-check)
- `run.cache: true` (task caching)
- `resolve.alias { "vite-plus/test": "vitest" }` (workaround for VP v0.2.5 CJS shim bug — shim uses `require()` but vitest 4.x is ESM-only)

No package-level `vite.config.ts` in `packages/extension/` — deleted during consolidation.

### Working commands
| Command | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| `vp check` | ✅ Pass | Format + oxlint + tsc, 16 files |
| `vp check --fix` | ✅ Pass | Auto-fixes format + lint |
| `vp test` | ✅ Pass | Root or from extension dir, 1 test |
| `vp format` | ✅ | oxfmt |
| `vp lint` | ✅ | oxlint with type-aware rules |
| `vp install` | ✅ | pnpm workspace install |
| `vp config` | ✅ | Installed `.vite-hooks/`, set git hooksPath |

### Structures
```
.                              # repo root
  packages/extension/          # extension source (pi-agent loaded)
    package.json               # "pi": { "extensions": ["./src/index.ts"] } TBD
    src/
      index.ts                 # entry point (placeholder: "Hello, tsdown!")
    tests/
      index.test.ts            # vitest stub (imports from "vite-plus/test")
  .vite-hooks/                 # pre-commit hooks (installed by vp config)
  .github/
    PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
  AGENTS.md                    # updated for VP + gh workflow
```

### Symlink (run once)
```bash
ln -s "$PWD/packages/extension" ~/.pi/agent/extensions/cccc-bridge
```

### PR workflow
- Use `gh pr create` (not tea)
- PR template at `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`
- AGENTS.md has full workflow details

### Known issues
- VP v0.2.5 `vp create` sub-templates (application, library) fail with exit 243 in non-interactive mode when `vite-plus` isn't resolvable. Root monorepo files are generated before the failure. Workaround: install deps first, then templates work.
- `vite-plus/test` CJS shim can't `require("vitest")` because vitest 4.x is ESM-only. Workaround: root `vite.config.ts` has `resolve.alias { "vite-plus/test": "vitest" }`.

### Next task: kg9.2 — Implement CCCC daemon client wrapper
close_reason: Completed: VP toolchain installed, monorepo scaffolded, extension package generated, vp check/test passing, git hooks active

title: Implement CCCC daemon client wrapper
description: Wrap the cccc-sdk CCCCClient to manage daemon connection, endpoint discovery, and provide a clean interface for actor_add, inbox_list, inbox_mark_read. This is the transport layer all other tasks depend on.
design:
Verified against installed cccc-sdk@0.4.4 (npm published version, differs from ~/git/cccc-sdk local version 0.4.32).

The SDK's CCCCClient uses options objects, NOT positional args. The wrapper must match the SDK's actual API shape.

## Installed SDK API (verified 2026-07-21)

CCCCClient.create(options?: CCCCClientOptions): Promise<CCCCClient>
  CCCCClientOptions: { ccccHome?: string; endpoint?: DaemonEndpoint; timeoutMs?: number }
  DaemonEndpoint: { transport: "unix"|"tcp"|""; path: string; host: string; port: number }

client.actorAdd(options: ActorAddOptions): Promise<ActorAddResult>
  ActorAddOptions: { groupId: string; actorId?: string; title?: string; runtime?: string; runner?: string; ... }
  ActorAddResult: { actor_id: string }

client.inboxList(options: InboxListOptions): Promise<InboxListResult>
  InboxListOptions: { groupId: string; actorId: string; by?: string; limit?: number; kindFilter?: string }
  InboxListResult: { messages: CCCSEvent[]; cursor?: { event_id: string; ts: string } }

client.inboxMarkRead(groupId: string, actorId: string, eventId: string, by?: string): Promise<...>

CCCSEvent: { id: string; ts: string; kind: string; group_id: string; scope_key?: string; by?: string; data: Record<string, unknown> }

## Wrapper Design

Create a CCCCBridgeClient class in src/client.ts that wraps CCCCClient:

```typescript
interface BridgeClientConfig {
  host: string;      // default from CCCC_DAEMON_HOST env, fallback "192.168.7.163"
  port: number;     // default from CCCC_DAEMON_PORT env, fallback 9765
  timeoutMs: number; // default 30000
}

class CCCCBridgeClient {
  private client: CCCCClient | null;

  async connect(config: BridgeClientConfig): Promise<void>
  disconnect(): void
  async registerActor(params: { groupId: string; actorId: string; runtime?: string; runner?: string; title?: string }): Promise<{ actorId: string }>
  async inboxList(params: { groupId: string; actorId: string; limit?: number }): Promise<CCCSEvent[]>
  async inboxMarkRead(params: { groupId: string; actorId: string; eventId: string }): Promise<void>
}
```

Key decisions:
- Wrapper accepts flat params objects (consistent across methods, easier to call)
- registerActor wraps actorAdd, returns only actorId (not full ActorAddResult) — caller doesn't need the event
- inboxList returns just the messages array (not the full InboxListResult) — cursor is internal to the wrapper
- inboxMarkRead wraps the SDK's positional-arg method into params-object style for consistency
- Connection errors are caught, logged via console.error, and re-thrown as a typed BridgeClientError so the caller can distinguish transport errors from API errors
- The wrapper does NOT swallow errors — it logs and re-throws. The session_start handler (Task 5) is responsible for graceful degradation.

## Testing Strategy

Tests must NOT hit the live daemon. Use dependency injection: the wrapper accepts an optional CCCCClient instance for testing, or construct a mock.

Approach: inject CCCCClient via constructor or connect() so tests can pass a stub. The SDK class is concrete (not interface-based), so create a lightweight interface that the wrapper depends on, then mock that interface in tests.

Define an internal interface matching the SDK methods we use:
```typescript
interface CCCCClientLike {
  actorAdd(options: ActorAddOptions): Promise<ActorAddResult>;
  inboxList(options: InboxListOptions): Promise<InboxListResult>;
  inboxMarkRead(groupId: string, actorId: string, eventId: string, by?: string): Promise<unknown>;
}
```

The wrapper depends on CCCCClientLike, not CCCCClient directly. At runtime, CCCCClient satisfies this interface structurally.

Tests create a mock implementing CCCCClientLike and verify:
- connect() creates a client (or uses injected one)
- registerActor() calls actorAdd with correct params and returns actorId
- inboxList() calls inboxList with correct params and returns messages array
- inboxMarkRead() calls inboxMarkRead with correct params
- disconnect() cleans up
- Errors from the SDK are re-thrown as BridgeClientError
acceptance_criteria:
src/client.ts exists with CCCCBridgeClient class
CCCCBridgeClient provides: connect(config), disconnect(), registerActor(params), inboxList(params), inboxMarkRead(params)
Wrapper depends on CCCCClientLike interface (not CCCCClient concrete class) for testability
Connection config read from env: CCCC_DAEMON_HOST (default 192.168.7.163), CCCC_DAEMON_PORT (default 9765)
SDK errors are caught, logged via console.error, and re-thrown as BridgeClientError
Unit tests cover: connect, registerActor, inboxList, inboxMarkRead, disconnect, and error wrapping
Tests use a mock implementing CCCCClientLike — NO live daemon calls in tests
vp check passes with zero errors
vp test passes with all tests green
notes:
IMPORTANT for implementer: The installed npm package cccc-sdk@0.4.4 differs from the local ~/git/cccc-sdk (v0.4.32). Use the INSTALLED version (0.4.4). Verify API by reading node_modules/cccc-sdk/dist/types.d.ts and client.d.ts if signatures are unclear.

The SDK's CCCCClient.create() accepts endpoint config with {transport: "tcp", host, port}. For TCP connections, set transport to "tcp" and provide host/port. The SDK auto-discovers via daemon descriptor file if no endpoint is given, but for our use case we always have explicit host/port from env.

Import paths: `import { CCCCClient } from "cccc-sdk"` — the package is ESM-only (type: module, exports field).

Do NOT use before_agent_start or any pi extension API in this task. This task is pure client wrapper code — no extension integration. That is Task 5.

comments.author: czer323
comments.text: Implementation complete. See commit 41a63b2 for details.

title: Implement actor registration and identity management
description: On session_start, auto-register a persistent CCCC actor (runtime=custom, runner=headless). Cache actor_id to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/cccc-bridge-state.json. Support env override (CCCC_ACTOR_ID) and .pi config override.
design:
Identity resolution order:
1. CCCC_ACTOR_ID env var (explicit override)
2. .pi config cccc-bridge.actorId (project-local override)
3. Cached actor_id from ~/.pi/agent/extensions/cccc-bridge-state.json
4. Auto-generate: pi-<hostname>-<short-hash-of-cwd> and register via actor_add

State file format (cccc-bridge-state.json):
{
  "actor_id": "pi-truenas-a1b2c3",
  "group_id": "g_c8878957bd2c",
  "registered_at": "2026-07-21T00:00:00Z"
}

Registration call: client.registerActor(groupId, actorId, runtime="custom", runner="headless")
If actor already exists (daemon returns error), treat as success (idempotent).
acceptance_criteria:
On first run, actor is auto-registered via actor_add with runtime=custom, runner=headless
Actor_id is cached to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/cccc-bridge-state.json
On subsequent runs, cached actor_id is reused (no duplicate registration)
Env var CCCC_ACTOR_ID overrides auto-generation
.pi config can specify actor_id
Graceful handling when actor already exists (idempotent registration)
notes:
See ~/git/cccc-sdk/spec/CCCC_DAEMON_IPC_V1.md lines 2068-2099 for actor_add spec.
Runtime type should be "custom" (not web_model as the idea doc suggested).
Runner should be "headless" (daemon will not try to spawn/manage the process).
The actor persists in the CCCC group even when the agent is offline.

title: Implement inbox polling and message delivery
description: Poll inbox_list on an interval from session_start. On new messages, call pi.sendMessage({triggerTurn: true}) to deliver into the session. Deduplicate by event_id. Mark messages read via inbox_mark_read after delivery.
design:
Polling loop:
- setInterval-based poller started from session_start
- Each tick: call client.inboxList(groupId, actorId)
- For each new message (by event_id not in seen set):
  - Call pi.sendMessage({ customType: "cccc-inbox", content: formattedMessage, display: true, details: { groupId, eventId, from, text } }, { triggerTurn: true })
  - Add event_id to seen set
  - Call client.inboxMarkRead(groupId, actorId, eventId)

Message formatting:
  "New CCCC message from <by>:\n\n<text>"

Seen set: in-memory Set<string>, cleared on session_shutdown.
Polling interval: CCCC_POLL_INTERVAL_MS env var, default 3000.
acceptance_criteria:
Polling loop starts on session_start and runs at configurable interval (default 3s)
inbox_list is called each cycle to fetch unread messages for the actor
New messages are delivered via pi.sendMessage with triggerTurn=true and customType=cccc-inbox
Messages are deduplicated by event_id (never delivered twice)
After delivery, inbox_mark_read is called for each delivered message
Polling stops cleanly on session_shutdown
notes:
See ~/git/agent-talk/extensions/inbox-monitor.ts for the delivery pattern (pi.sendMessage with triggerTurn).
See ~/git/cccc-sdk/ts/examples/stream.ts for inbox_list usage.
The agent-talk pattern uses a file spool + fs.watch; we use direct daemon polling instead, which is simpler for Phase 1.
Phase 2 replaces polling with events_stream for real-time push.

title: Wire up session lifecycle (session_start, session_shutdown)
description: Connect the pieces: session_start opens daemon connection, registers actor, starts polling. session_shutdown stops polling, closes connection. Handle graceful degradation when daemon is unreachable.
acceptance_criteria:
session_start handler connects to daemon, registers actor, starts polling
session_shutdown handler stops polling, closes daemon connection
Graceful degradation: if daemon is unreachable, notify user via ctx.ui.notify and continue without CCCC (session still works)
Extension is inert when no group_id is configured (no errors, no connection attempts)
ctx.ui.setStatus used to show CCCC connection status in the footer
notes:
See ~/git/pi/packages/coding-agent/docs/extensions.md lines 222-224: "Do not start background resources from the factory. Defer to session_start."
See ~/git/agent-talk/extensions/inbox-monitor.ts lines 158-171 for the session_start/session_shutdown pattern.
ctx.ui.setStatus shows a footer status indicator.
The extension must not crash the session if CCCC is unreachable.

title: Test end-to-end against live CCCC daemon
description: Verify the full flow against the CCCC daemon at 192.168.7.163:9765: extension loads, actor registers, messages from Web UI arrive in pi session, messages marked read, clean shutdown.
design:
Manual test procedure:
1. Start pi with the extension installed: CCCC_GROUP_ID=g_c8878957bd2c pi
2. Verify actor appears in CCCC Web UI actor list
3. Send a message from Web UI addressed to the actor
4. Verify message appears in pi session and triggers a turn
5. Verify agent's response (agent uses CCCC MCP tools to reply, or just observe the message arrived)
6. Exit pi and verify clean shutdown

Test against the live daemon at 192.168.7.163:9765.
Document any issues found and file as follow-up issues.
acceptance_criteria:
Extension loads in a pi session without errors
Actor appears in CCCC Web UI at http://192.168.7.163:8848/ui/
Message sent from CCCC Web UI arrives in the pi session within one polling cycle (~3s)
Agent responds to the message (verifying triggerTurn works)
Message is marked read in CCCC (visible in Web UI)
Session exit closes the connection cleanly (no errors in pi or daemon logs)
notes:
This is the MVP checkpoint. If this passes, Phase 1 is complete.
The agent's reply path depends on CCCC MCP tools being configured separately (open question 4).
For testing, the agent can also be asked to reply via a bash command calling cccc-sdk directly.

title: Add events_stream support (persistent TCP, replace polling)
description: Replace inbox_list polling with events_stream persistent TCP connection. Manage reconnect, heartbeat, backpressure. Use cccc-sdk async iterator. Fall back to polling if stream fails.
design:
Replace setInterval polling with client.eventsStream({ groupId, by: actorId, kinds: ["chat.message"] }).
The cccc-sdk returns an async iterator over EventStreamItem.

Implementation:
- Open stream in session_start (after actor registration)
- Consume iterator in a background async loop
- On heartbeat: ignore
- On event: deliver via pi.sendMessage, mark read
- On error/disconnect: attempt reconnect with backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s)
- After N failed reconnects (default 5): fall back to polling mode

The stream uses the same seen-event_id dedup as polling.
acceptance_criteria:
events_stream persistent TCP connection opened at session_start
Incoming chat.message events delivered in real-time (sub-second latency)
Heartbeat events handled (ignored, not delivered)
Reconnect logic on disconnect with exponential backoff
Falls back to inbox_list polling if stream fails after max retries
No duplicate delivery between stream and polling fallback
notes:
See ~/git/cccc-sdk/spec/CCCC_DAEMON_IPC_V1.md section 4.5 and 8.15 for events_stream spec.
See ~/git/cccc-sdk/ts/examples/stream.ts for usage.
The daemon MAY drop slow subscribers (spec line 3305); clients must reconnect and reconcile.

title: Add multi-group support per session
description: Allow a single pi session to join multiple CCCC groups simultaneously. Maintain separate inbox/polling per group. Tag delivered messages with source group.
design:
Refactor BridgeState to manage a Map<groupId, GroupConnection>.
Each GroupConnection owns: actorId, polling timer (or stream), seenIds set.
session_start iterates configured group_ids and establishes a connection per group.
Messages delivered with details.groupId so the agent (and UI) can distinguish sources.
acceptance_criteria:
Session can join multiple CCCC groups simultaneously
Each group has its own actor registration, inbox, and polling/stream
Delivered messages are tagged with source group_id in the message details
Group list is configurable via env (CCCC_GROUP_IDS=g1,g2) or .pi config
notes:
See ~/git/cccc-sdk/spec/CCCC_DAEMON_IPC_V1.md: actor_add requires group_id, actors are scoped per group.
Each group needs its own actor registration.

title: Add group auto-discovery from cwd/git repo
description: On session_start, call groups() to list CCCC groups. Match cwd/git repo against group scopes. Join matching group(s), fall back to default lobby group.
design:
Discovery flow:
1. Get cwd and git toplevel (via child_process.execSync("git rev-parse --show-toplevel"))
2. Call client.groups() to list all groups
3. For each group, call group_show to get scope info
4. Match group scopes against cwd/git toplevel
5. Join matching group(s); if none match, join CCCC_DEFAULT_GROUP_ID (env, default lobby)

This runs before actor registration in session_start.
acceptance_criteria:
On session_start, extension calls groups() to list available CCCC groups
If a group has a scope matching the cwd or git repo root, it is joined automatically
If no matching group exists, falls back to a configured default lobby group
Discovery results are logged for debugging
notes:
See ~/git/cccc-sdk/spec/CCCC_DAEMON_IPC_V1.md: group_use sets active scope for a group using a path.
Scopes are attached paths (git repos). This maps naturally to cwd-based discovery.

title: Sub-agent auto-registration and parent announcement
description: When extension detects a sub-agent session (via env var), auto-register a derived actor_id and message the parent's actor to announce readiness. Parent knows child id from the announcement.
design:
Sub-agent detection:
- Check for PI_PARENT_SESSION or PI_TASK_PARENT env var (need to verify which exists across harnesses)
- If present, this is a sub-agent session

Derived actor_id:
- Read parent actor_id from parent session state or env
- Generate: parent-actor-id + "-child-" + short-hash
- Register with runtime=custom, runner=headless

Announcement:
- After registration, call client.send({ groupId, actorId: childId, to: [parentActorId], text: "Sub-agent ready: <childId>" })
- Parent sees this as an incoming CCCC message and knows the child's actor_id

Cleanup:
- On session_shutdown, optionally call actor_remove or actor_update to mark as inactive
acceptance_criteria:
Extension detects sub-agent sessions via env var (PI_PARENT_SESSION or equivalent)
Sub-agent auto-registers a derived actor_id (e.g., parent-id-child)
Sub-agent sends announcement message to parent actor via CCCC send
Parent receives announcement and knows child actor_id
Sub-agent de-registers on session_shutdown
notes:
Need to research how OMP/pi-agent propagates env to subagents.
See ~/git/oh-my-pi/packages/coding-agent/src/task/executor.ts for in-process subagent execution.
See ~/git/oh-my-pi/docs/task-agent-discovery.md for agent definition and spawning.
OMP's AgentRegistry assigns stable ids to subagents; the CCCC actor should mirror this.
This is Phase 3, not critical for MVP.

title: Descriptive dynamic actor IDs (role + machine + project)
description: Replace generic actor IDs with descriptive names encoding role, machine, and project context. e.g. pi-truenas-cccc, research-truenas-hermes, infra-truenas-opencode.
design:
Actor ID formula: <role>-<machine>-<project>
- role: from .pi config or env (CCCC_AGENT_ROLE), default "pi"
- machine: hostname shortened (e.g., "truenas" from "truenas.local")
- project: git repo basename or cwd basename

Examples:
- pi-truenas-pi-agent-cccc (this project)
- research-truenas-hermes (hermes doing research)
- infra-truenas-arcane (infrastructure agent)

Cached in state file. Updated when project context changes.
acceptance_criteria:
Actor IDs encode role, machine, and project context
IDs are human-readable (e.g., pi-truenas-cccc, research-truenas-hermes)
Role is derived from agent type or project config
Machine is derived from hostname
Project is derived from git repo name or cwd basename
notes:
User explicitly does NOT want firstmate's crew/captain terminology.
Use existing CCCC terms (foreman, peer) where role differentiation is needed.
The formula should be workshoped before implementation (open question 1).

title: Cross-group messaging awareness (Group Bridge)
description: Integrate with CCCC Group Bridge for cross-context messaging. Support send_cross_group and handle cross-group receipt events. Enable hermes-to-foreman and similar cross-group flows.
design:
Integrate CCCC Group Bridge:
- Subscribe to chat.cross_group_receipt events in addition to chat.message
- Include src_group_id and src_event_id in delivered message details
- Ensure CCCC MCP tools include send_cross_group capability
- Display Group Bridge status in ctx.ui.setStatus

This enables the hermes-to-foreman cross-context messaging use case.
acceptance_criteria:
Extension handles chat.cross_group_receipt events
Agent can send cross-group messages via CCCC MCP send_cross_group tool
Cross-group messages are delivered with source group provenance
Group Bridge pairing status is visible in extension status
notes:
See ~/git/cccc-sdk/spec/CCCC_DAEMON_IPC_V1.md: send_cross_group (section 8.6, line 2563).
See ~/git/cccc/docs/guide/group-bridge.md for Group Bridge setup.
Group Bridge is a CCCC v0.4.29+ feature.
